entries.text: Hemiola is a multi-platform music app that lets you: play a piano keyboard, connect MIDI instruments, upload your own SoundFont files, create analog synth sounds, add audio effects, configure external MIDI devices, and use it as a MIDI router — with the same experience across all platforms.

Hemiola is free on mobile. The Free version allows up to 3 presets per section. A one-time Pro upgrade (iOS/Android) removes that limit. Cloud subscriptions unlock additional features and supersede the one-time purchase for the duration of the subscription — see the Cloud Account section for details.
Cloud features include automatic backup, preset sync across devices, a Community Library for browsing and publishing shared presets, bookmark syncing, push notifications, and a public profile.
Presets & Scenes

A preset icon (library/music) is always visible in the top bar. It is grey and disabled in sections that have no preset support. It turns accent-coloured when presets are available and the current state is saved; it turns orange when there are unsaved changes (dirty state).
Preset dialog (clean): tap a preset to apply it immediately. Tap the edit icon to rename, or the delete icon to remove a user preset. The default "—" preset cannot be modified or deleted.
Preset dialog (dirty): tap a user preset to overwrite it with current values, or use the Add New button to save as a new named preset. A Discard option resets to the last saved state.

Sections with preset support: Piano Keyboard (scale, scroll position, pressure, haptic), Sound (analog synth + effects), Configuration Tool device pages, and MIDI Mapper (full mapping list with active/inactive state).
The SCENE preset saves the Sound preset, limiter, SoundFont, MIDI Mapper, and device plugin parameters at once — independent of individual section presets.
App Bar
The app bar at the top contains persistent actions available on every screen.
Hemiola logo → Home screen; also acts as a Back button when inside a sub-screen.

Bell icon (Push Notifications) → shown for both signed-in users and guests; displays a red badge with the count of unread notifications. Tap to open the notifications panel directly below the icon. See the Push Notifications section for details.
Community Library icon (cloud) → opens the shared preset library where you can browse, apply, import, and bookmark presets published by the community. Appears grey with a bar when the app is offline.
Scenes icon → save and restore a full snapshot of all Sound Generator and MIDI settings in one step
Preset icon (library/music) → always visible; grey when the current section has no preset support, accent-coloured when clean, orange when there are unsaved changes
Battery icon → shown only while Config Tool is the active tab, for compatible devices
Bottom Tab Bar
The bottom bar gives quick access to the four main sections.
Piano icon → Piano Keyboard
Link icon (Config Tool) → accent-coloured when a supported device is connected; long-tap to activate plugin Developer/Sandbox mode
MIDI icon → MIDI Device Manager; accent when a device is connected; long-tap opens the MIDI Console directly
Equalizer icon (Sound Generator) → accent when the generator is playing; long-tap to toggle on/off
Knobs
Drag up or right to increase the value; drag down or left to decrease
Tap the arc to jump directly to that position
Double-tap the knob to reset to the default value
Long-press the value label to open a modal editor for exact numeric entry.

Long-press the knob body (hold without moving) to enter Fine mode: the drag sensitivity is reduced 10×, allowing very precise adjustments. A red border appears on the knob body while Fine mode is active. Fine mode deactivates automatically when you release.
App Updates
Store Updates (App Store / Play Store): new features and bug fixes
In-app Updates: embedded SoundFont files, new configuration tool device pages, and Help system updates
When an in-app update installs a new supported device plugin, the device catalog is refreshed immediately without restarting the app.
An alert is shown at startup when updates are available. You can also check manually: Home → About Hemiola → "Check for updates".

Sign in or create an account directly from the Home screen by tapping the account icon. An active cloud subscription unlocks additional features and supersedes the one-time app purchase for as long as the subscription is maintained.
Subscription Tiers

Feature|Free App|Pro App|Cloud Free|Cloud Pro
Piano Keyboard|✓|✓|✓|✓
Drum Pads|✓|✓|✓|✓
Analog & SoundFont Synths|✓|✓|✓|✓
Step Sequencer|✓|✓|✓|✓
Presets per section|3 max|Unlimited|3 max|Unlimited
Preview Community Presets|✓|✓|✓|✓
Download Community Presets|—|✓|—|✓
Share your presets|-|-|-|✓
Config Tool & MIDI (mobile)|✓|✓|✓|✓
Config Tool & MIDI (desktop)|—|—|-|✓
Live Mode (mobile)|—|✓|—|✓
Live Mode (desktop)|—|—|—|✓
Settings backup|—|—|Latest only|✓
Backup snapshots|—|—|-|Up to 10
Multi-device sync|—|—|Up to 2|Up to 5

App Pro = one-time in-app purchase (iOS/Android). Cloud Pro is a recurring subscription (monthly or annual). An active cloud subscription grants at least Pro-level app features on all platforms — including desktop — for the duration of the subscription. If the subscription lapses, the tier reverts to the last purchased one-time level.
Account Management
Profile tab: edit your display name, profile photo, and public profile settings — enabling public profile allows the community to see your shared presets
Subscription tab: view your current tier, view and manage your subscription
My Devices tab: lists all devices registered to your account with their last-seen timestamp
Backup tab: view the current backup status, back up now, or restore from the latest snapshot (Cloud Free) — or configure the automatic backup interval and manage multiple snapshots (Cloud Pro)
Shared Presets tab: manage your published presets (edit, hide, or delete). Requires Cloud Pro
Login tab: optionally save credentials securely on the device for faster sign-in; saved credentials are shown with a dedicated indicator and can be removed with Forget Credentials
Password reset and account deletion are available from the account page
Shared Presets
Publish your current Sound or device settings as a community preset so other users can browse and download it. Requires Cloud Pro.
Enable Public Profile (Profile tab) to make your published presets visible to the community — presets marked public but with a non-public profile show a warning icon
Each published preset can be hidden (without deleting it) or deleted permanently from the Shared Presets tab
You can edit the name, description, and hashtags of a published preset at any time
The profile is refreshed automatically each time you switch tabs in the Cloud Account dialog, so changes made on another device are always reflected.
Settings Backup

Cloud backup of your settings and presets, configurable from the Cloud Account dialog → Backup tab. Cloud Free: manual backup, latest snapshot only. Cloud Pro: automatic backup with configurable interval and up to 10 snapshots.

Automatic Backup interval (Backup tab): choose Disabled, As Soon As Possible (30 s), After 5/15/30 min of inactivity, or After 1 hour of inactivity. The backup fires after the selected quiet period following the last change — not on a fixed schedule

Only the differences since the last backup are transmitted (delta backup), keeping uploads fast and bandwidth-efficient. The baseline persists across restarts, so no redundant upload is made at app launch if nothing has changed
Each backup creates a named snapshot. Browse the full history and restore any previous state from the Backup tab
Backups are per-device: each registered device has its own independent backup history
Manual backup is also available from the Backup tab at any time. After a restore, the next automatic backup is always a full upload to reflect the restored state.
Device Registration

Each device running Hemiola is automatically registered to your account when you sign in with an active Cloud subscription. Registration enables per-device backup and lets you manage your devices via the cloud.
Devices are identified by a unique UUID generated and stored locally on the device
The device name is set automatically based on the platform (iOS, Android, macOS, etc.)
Tap any device in the My Devices list to open Device Settings, where you can rename the device, or access Pro device management features
If you uninstall and reinstall the app, or install on a new device, a new UUID is generated and the device appears as a new entry in your account
Cloud Free accounts can register up to 2 devices; Cloud Pro accounts can register up to 5. If you reach the limit, remove an existing device from My Devices before signing in on a new one
Cloud Free users who reach the 2-device limit are prompted to remove a device or upgrade to Cloud Pro (which allows up to 5 registered devices).
Device Management (Cloud Pro)
Cloud Pro subscribers can manage their devices directly from Device Settings.
Delete Device: permanently remove a device and all its cloud backups from your account. This cannot be undone

Transfer Presets: selectively copy presets between two devices. Choose the direction (from or to this device), then pick preset groups and individual presets to transfer. Name conflicts are resolved automatically by appending the source device name in parentheses

Clone: completely replicate one device's configuration onto another. Choose the direction, then select Full Clone (settings + all presets) or Presets Only. Both modes overwrite the target — a double confirmation is required
After a reinstall or on a new device: sign in, then open Device Settings for the old device and use Clone to restore your previous settings and presets.

A Scene is a snapshot that captures your Sound preset (analog synth + effects), limiter, SoundFont configuration, MIDI Mapper, and device plugin parameters in a single preset. It is the quickest way to switch between completely different sound setups.
How to use
Tap the Scenes icon in the app bar to open the Scene list
Tap a scene name to apply it immediately — all covered parameters are updated at once
The icon turns orange when the current state differs from the saved scene (dirty); tap it to save over an existing scene or add a new one
Add New: saves the current state as a new named scene
Tap the edit icon next to a scene to rename it; tap the delete icon to remove it
Scenes are not shared to the cloud community — they are local to your device and backed up with your regular settings backup (Cloud Pro).
What a Scene holds
Sound Preset: analog synth parameters (oscillators, LFO, LPF, filter envelope, mono/glide/unison, ADSR, noise) plus effects (chorus, delay, reverb)
Limiter settings
SoundFont Synth: on/off, volume, pan, active SoundFont file, active patch
MIDI Mapper: full mapping list with active/inactive state
Device plugin parameters (if a device is connected via Configuration Tool)
Piano Keyboard settings (zoom, scroll position, pressure, haptic) are stored in separate Piano presets and are NOT included in a Scene.
Hemiola's keyboard spans 7 octaves with pressure sensitivity (on iOS and compatible Android devices) and multi-touch chord support.
The keyboard is displayed vertically on phones (portrait), and horizontally on landscape, tablet, and desktop layouts.
Navigation
On phones, the bottom tab bar is hidden whenever the keyboard layout is too compact (including portrait and compact landscape) to maximise the playable area.
When enough room is available (tablet, desktop, or wider phone layouts), the tab bar and the section name / help link are shown below the keyboard.
Top-bar Controls
Scroll arrows: tap to move one key, long-tap to move one full octave
Pressure toggle: enable or disable touch pressure (velocity) sensitivity. Experimental — may not work on all devices.
Haptic toggle: enable or disable vibration feedback on key press. Experimental — may not work on all devices.
Master volume slider: quick access to overall volume
Zoom in/out: adjust key width; long-tap the magnifier to snap to min or max zoom
Center icon: reset zoom to default; long-tap to center the active octave

Preset icon: save and recall Piano Keyboard configurations (scale, scroll position, pressure sensitivity, haptic feedback). Applying a preset immediately redraws the keyboard to the saved zoom and scroll position.
Windows Tablets: for best playability, disable the system option "Three- and four-finger touch movement" in Windows settings to avoid gesture conflicts while playing.

The Drum Pad is a 4×4 grid of 16 velocity-sensitive pads that send MIDI notes on channel 10 (GM Percussion). It is available as an alternative to the Piano Keyboard and uses only the SoundFont engine.
A SoundFont file must be loaded and active. The selected SoundFont must be General MIDI (GM) compatible — it needs a standard GM Percussion bank on channel 10 to produce drum sounds.
How To Use
Tap a pad to trigger the corresponding drum sound. Multi-touch is supported: you can hit multiple pads simultaneously.
Each pad has a unique color. When touched, the pad color brightens to give immediate visual feedback.
Toolbar Controls
Piano icon (top-left): exit Drum Pad mode and return to the Piano Keyboard.
Touch Sensitivity (mobile only): enable or disable touch pressure (velocity) sensitivity. Experimental — may not work on all devices.
Haptic Feedback (mobile only): enable or disable vibration on pad hit. Experimental — may not work on all devices.

Shake To Play (mobile only): assign a drum pad to the shake gesture. Experimental — may not work on all devices. Tap the icon to enter assignment mode — a message appears asking you to tap a pad. Once assigned, each shake sounds that pad with velocity-sensitive dynamics (monophonic: previous note stops before the new one plays). Tap the icon again to cancel. The icon turns amber while waiting for a pad selection, and accent color when a pad is assigned. Long-press the icon to open Shake Settings: adjust sensitivity (lower = more sensitive), enable Wrist Rejection to automatically filter wrist rebounds, and control velocity sensitivity (higher = wider dynamic range).
Mute / Volume Down / Volume Up: control the master output volume.
Help icon (top-right): open this help section.
Windows Tablets: for best playability, disable the system option "Three- and four-finger touch movement" in Windows settings to avoid gesture conflicts while playing.

The Step Sequencer has 16 drum tracks and a Melody / Bass track. It programs up to 32 steps of GM percussion plus a monophonic melodic line. It runs in the background: switching to other tabs does not interrupt playback.
A SoundFont file must be loaded and active. The selected SoundFont must be GM-compatible — it needs a standard GM Percussion bank on channel 10.
Transport
Play / Stop: tap the play button to start playback from step 1. Tap again to stop. The active playhead column is highlighted.

Record: tap the Record button to enter recording mode. A dialog asks whether to record Drums or Melody / Bass. Playback starts automatically if stopped. In Drums mode, note-ons matching a drum row are written to the nearest step; a click track (Claves) sounds every quarter note — louder on beat 1. In Melody / Bass mode, notes played on the keyboard or via external MIDI are recorded as pitched events with velocity and duration. Tap Stop to exit recording.
Tap Tempo: tap repeatedly at the desired tempo to set BPM automatically. Hemiola averages the last few intervals to compute BPM. Taps older than 4 seconds are discarded.
Grid
The top row (amber) is the Melody / Bass track. Below it are the 16 drum rows, each corresponding to a GM percussion instrument.
Tap any drum cell to toggle a step on or off. Long-press a drum cell to toggle it as a silent step (greyed out): silent steps hold the loop position without producing sound.
Row labels: tap a label to mute or unmute that entire row (content is preserved, the row turns grey). Long-press a label to delete all steps in that row immediately, with no confirmation.
Melody / Bass Track
The melody row displays recorded notes as horizontal bars: vertical position reflects pitch, bar width reflects duration (steps), bar height reflects velocity.
Recording is monophonic: a new note overwrites any existing note at the same position and truncates any overlapping predecessor.
Long-press the Melody label to clear the melody track instantly, or use Clear Pattern with the Melody / Bass checkbox.
Parameters
BPM (40–240): set the tempo with the +/− spinner. Changes take effect immediately.
Steps (16 / 32): toggle between 16 and 32 steps. Switching to 32 pads existing steps; switching back to 16 truncates the last 16.
Time (4/4 · 3/4 · 6/8 · 5/4): visual grouping indicator only — does not affect playback timing.
Swing (50–100): amount of swing on odd steps. 50 = straight; higher values produce a shuffle feel.
Pattern Presets
Tap the Pattern chip to open the preset list. Factory patterns cover a variety of styles; you can save your own.
Clear Pattern: tap the trash icon to open a dialog with separate checkboxes for Drum Tracks and Melody / Bass. Only the selected tracks are cleared.
The Configuration Tool tab displays a native UI for the connected MIDI device. Connect a supported device and tap the Config tab to configure it.
How it works
Each supported device has its own plugin that renders directly inside Hemiola.
All parameter changes are sent to the device in real time.
Use the Preset icon in the top bar to save and recall your device's configuration. The icon is accent-coloured when clean and orange when there are unsaved changes.
When a plugin is active, an orange warning icon appears next to the bottom Configuration Tool title. Tap it to view the manufacturer disclaimer.
If no device is connected, the tab shows a "No supported device connected" placeholder. When a device is active, a device-specific help section appears at the bottom of this help screen.
Multiple Devices
When two or more supported devices are connected simultaneously, a device-switcher button appears next to the preset icon in the top bar.
Tap the Config tab icon again (while it is already active) to open the device-switcher menu and select which device to configure
The menu shows all currently recognised devices; the active one is highlighted
Each device keeps its own independent preset list
Supported Devices
Tahorng Windkey — melodica-style digital wind instrument
Akai EWI USB — digital wind controller
Roland AE-01 Aerophone — digital wind instrument
ClariiMini — digital wind instrument
Moog Theremini — digital theremin
Open Source
Device plugins are open source. You can propose new devices, report issues, or contribute plugins on GitHub: https://github.com/MrMep/hemiola-device-plugins
Help pages are also open source. You can suggest improvements or contribute translations on GitHub: https://github.com/MrMep/hemiola-help
The MIDI Device Manager lists all available MIDI devices. Tap a device to connect or disconnect it.
Bluetooth devices: BT scanning is off by default; tap the BT icon and grant permission to scan
Network switch (iOS/macOS): enables Network MIDI sessions
Virtual switch: enables a virtual MIDI port (availability depends on platform)
Reload button: fully shuts down the MIDI subsystem and restarts it — useful if devices are not detected or the MIDI stack appears stuck. Last-known devices are reconnected automatically.
Use the bottom bar buttons to access MIDI Mapper. Some general MIDI settings are also available in the main Settings window.
General MIDI preferences (SoundFont/Analog channel, MIDI Thru, Pitch Bend Range) are configured in the Preferences dialog. See the Settings section for details.
MIDI Mapper
Lets you control Hemiola parameters via MIDI. Mappings are organised in three categories:
User — custom mappings you create to forward or transform MIDI messages
Synth — built-in mappings for Sound Generator parameters (volume, oscillators, effects, etc.)
UI — built-in mappings for interface controls (show/hide sections, tab switching)
Tap an entry to activate or deactivate it
Use the toggle buttons to filter the list by category; "activate all visible" applies to the filtered set
Add custom mapping: define name, in/out channel, command, and data; tap the settings icon to edit or delete

Test Mode (ear icon): when enabled, incoming MIDI messages are not executed but instead the matching mappings flash three times and the list scrolls to show them. Useful for verifying your external controller setup.
Wildcards & Device Filters
The MIDI Mapper acts as a powerful router and rewriter. You can use 'OMNI' and 'ANY' values to match multiple messages, and 'SAME AS IN' to pass them through unchanged:
Device Filters: restrict a mapping to listen from or send to a specific connected device, or leave as 'ANY DEVICE' / 'ALL DEVICES'.
Channel/Command: set the input to 'OMNI' or 'ALL MSGS' to intercept any incoming stream, and set the output to 'SAME AS IN' to preserve the original property.
Data 1: enter '-1' to match any note or CC value, passing it through dynamically.
Long-tap the Add button to reset all mappings to defaults and delete all custom entries.
MIDI Autopilot
Two independent autopilot modes can be toggled separately in Settings → General:
Controls Autopilot: the app automatically switches tabs and scrolls to the affected controls when MIDI parameter changes are received through built-in mappings.
Keyboard Autopilot: when a MIDI note-on reaches the synth, the app switches to the piano tab and scrolls to show the played note.
Built-in mappings for a section that is currently switched off (e.g. Reverb, Analog Synth) are silently ignored.
MIDI Console

Displays a real-time list of recently received and sent MIDI messages. Fully supports SysEx payloads (up to 200 recent messages). Features hexagonal views, text decoding, clock omission, and quick log exports.
Access: long-tap the MIDI icon in the bottom tab bar, or long-tap the MIDI icon inside the MIDI Device Manager screen
Use the top toggles to pause scrolling, clear the list, switch to hexadecimal view, decode payload data, filter out clock signals, or export the log for troubleshooting.
Wind Controllers

Hemiola can detect wind MIDI controllers (EWI, Roland Aerophone, Yamaha WX, Warbl, Sylphyo, etc.) and route their breath CC directly to the audio output volume, bypassing the standard MIDI pipeline for a more responsive feel.
When a known wind controller connects for the first time, a detection dialog appears asking which CC carries the breath signal (CC 2, CC 11, CC 7, or Try All).
You can also enable Wind Controller mode manually: tap the settings icon next to any device and check the Wind Controller checkbox at the top of the dialog.
Advanced — Response Curve: choose Linear, Exponential, or Logarithmic response and adjust Curvature (-1.0 to +1.0) for precise feel. Changes are applied in real time.
The wind controller breath path operates before the MIDI Mapper. CC 7 (Channel Volume), if selected as the breath source, is intercepted and does not reach the SoundFont engine.

The Sound Generator includes two independent synthesizers: SoundFont and Analog. It responds to the piano keyboard, USB/BT MIDI devices, the virtual interface, and MIDI Network (iOS/macOS). Audio buffer size is configurable for minimum latency.
MAIN
Master Volume: overall output level
Channel Volume: volume sent via MIDI CC 7
Pan: stereo panning (left ↔ right)
Stereo mode: select mono or stereo output
A red dot appears on the top bar when clipping is detected. Lower the volume or activate the Limiter (Sound Effects) to resolve it.
PITCH
Master Tuning: fine-tune in cents (−100 to +100); the A4 reference frequency is displayed in Hz
Transpose: shift pitch by −12 to +12 semitones
A4 switch: emits a reference A4 tone, affected by Transpose and Master Tuning
AUDIO OUTPUT
Buffer Size: larger values reduce under-runs but increase latency
AUTO: automatic buffer size management (recommended in most cases)

Windows Exclusive Mode: gives Hemiola direct access to one output device for the lowest latency. While active, other apps on the same device are muted. If the device disconnects or is busy, Hemiola automatically falls back to shared mode. On multi-channel devices (4+ channels), you can select the active stereo pair (0-1, 2-3, 4-5, ...) in real time from the Exclusive panel. Audio is routed only to the selected pair; all other channels stay silent. The Release On Minimise option controls whether Hemiola releases exclusive ownership while in background and automatically reclaims it on resume. If exclusive activation fails, or if exclusive is re-enabled and still silent, tap Send Logs in the Exclusive panel and share the diagnostics report from the dialog.

If the Sound Engine icon turns red, an audio buffer under-run occurred — increase the buffer size or enable AUTO. The section also shows a best-effort estimate of current audio latency and the sampling rate (set automatically by the audio engine).

On first launch, Hemiola automatically runs an audio engine calibration that measures your device's latency, stability, and CPU performance, then configures the optimal settings. You can re-run calibration at any time by tapping the badge on the home screen.
SoundFont Synth
A sample-based synthesizer using the sf2 file format. Many free SoundFont collections are available online and can be uploaded to Hemiola.
Volume: output level of the SoundFont module
Pan: stereo panning of the SoundFont module
Current: select the active SoundFont file (when multiple are loaded)
Patch: select an instrument preset within the current SoundFont
Custom: upload a local .sf2 file to Hemiola
Analog Synth
A digital emulation of a classic analog synthesizer, loosely inspired by the Minimoog Model D.
Volume: output level of the Analog module
Pan: stereo panning of the Analog module
Analog Oscillators
All three oscillators share the same set of parameters:
Wave: waveform shape — Sine, Square, Sawtooth (×2), Triangle (×2), Pulse
Range: base octave — 32', 16', 8', 4', or 2'
Detune: fine-tune offset in cents (−100 to +100)
Shift: semitone offset (−12 to +12)
Volume: individual oscillator output level
PW: pulse width (duty cycle) — only affects the Pulse waveform. 50% = square wave, lower/higher values produce thinner pulses
Low Frequency Oscillator (LFO)
Route: Vibrato, Frequency (pitch), LPF Cutoff, or Stereo spread
Wave: waveform of the LFO (same types as oscillators)
BPM: oscillation frequency
Depth: amplitude range of the modulation
Low Pass Filter
A 4-pole (24 dB/oct) Moog Ladder filter applied per voice. At high emphasis the filter self-oscillates, producing a pitched tone.
Cutoff: frequencies above this threshold are attenuated
Emphasis: boosts frequencies around the cutoff frequency (resonance) — at maximum the filter self-oscillates
Amount: depth of filter envelope modulation (0 = none, 1000 = full sweep)
Kbd Track: keyboard tracking — how much the cutoff follows the played note (Off, 33%, 67%, 100%)
LPF Cutoff can be modulated by the LFO (Route: LPF Cutoff) or by MIDI CC.
Filter Envelope
A dedicated per-voice ADSR envelope that sweeps the filter cutoff over time. The sweep range is controlled by Amount in the LPF section.
Attack: time (ms) for the filter to open after note-on
Decay: time (ms) to fall from the peak to sustain level
Sustain: cutoff level while key is held (per mille, 0–1000)
Release: time (ms) for the filter to close after note-off
Mono / Glide
When Mono is enabled, only one note sounds at a time. Held notes are tracked in a stack — releasing the current note returns to the previous one.
Mono: toggle between monophonic (one note) and polyphonic (multiple notes) modes
Priority: which note sounds when multiple keys are held — Low (lowest note wins), High (highest), or Last (most recent)
Retrigger: when on, envelopes restart on each new note; when off, the pitch changes smoothly without retriggering (legato)
Glide: portamento time (ms) — how long the pitch takes to slide between notes. 0 = instant
Drift: analog oscillator drift — adds subtle random pitch instability to each voice, simulating analog VCO behavior (0 = perfectly tuned, 100 = maximum warmth)
Voices: number of unison voices per note (1–8). Multiple voices are detuned symmetrically for a thicker sound. Only works in Mono mode
Detune: spread in cents between unison voices (0–50). Higher values create a wider, more detuned chorus effect
Noise Generator
White or pink noise oscillator — useful for adding breath, air, or texture to a sound. White noise has equal energy at all frequencies; pink noise has more bass energy (−3 dB/octave)
Type: White or Pink noise
Volume: output level of the noise
Loudness Envelope (ADSR)
Controls the amplitude envelope of the Analog synth. Uses exponential (RC) curves for a natural, analog-like response.
Attack: time (ms) to reach full amplitude after note-on
Decay: time (ms) to fall to the sustain level
Sustain: amplitude level while key is held (per mille, 0–1000)
Release: time (ms) to fade to silence after note-off
Audio effects are applied in series: SoundFont + Analog → Chorus → Delay → Reverb → Limiter.
Chorus
Send: effect mix level (0–127; responds to MIDI CC 93)
Delay: delay time in milliseconds
Depth: modulation frequency range
Freq: LFO speed of the chorus modulation
Delay
BPM: repetition frequency
Decay: damping per repeat — 0 % = infinite repeat, 100 % = off
Amplitude: output level of the delay effect (%)
Reverb
8 presets: Vocal I, Vocal II, Bathroom, Small Room, Medium Room, Large Room, Church Hall, Cathedral.
Send: input level to the reverb (responds to MIDI CC 91)
Wet: output mix of the reverb signal (%)
Room Size: simulated room dimension (%)
Damp: high-frequency absorption (%)
Spread/Width: stereo width of the reverb tail (%)
Based on the Freeverb algorithm by Jezar, implemented via Dart Melty Sounds.
Limiter
Prevents clipping by compressing audio that exceeds the threshold. A sky-blue dot on the top bar indicates the limiter is engaged; a horizontal meter shows the current gain reduction.
Threshold: amplitude level at which compression begins (%)
Ceiling: maximum output amplitude after compression (%)
Attack: time (ms) to engage compression after threshold is crossed
Release: time (ms) to disengage compression after signal falls below threshold

The Community Library is a shared collection of presets published by Hemiola users. Open it from the cloud icon in the app bar. It is available to everyone — no subscription needed to browse and apply presets.
Browsing
Type in the search bar to filter presets by name or hashtag in real time
Use the sort chips below the search bar to change the order: Trending (default, ranked by the Hemiola Score algorithm), Recent (newest first), or Top (most voted of all time)
Scroll down to load more presets automatically (infinite scroll); pull to refresh the list

Each card shows the preset name, hashtags, author name (with a tier badge if earned), vote counts, and download count. Tap the card to expand it and see the full description, section chips, and action buttons
Tap the author name to view their profile (avatar, display name, username, bio)
Applying a preset
Apply is free and available to all users. It loads the preset values live without saving them locally — perfect for quick auditioning.
Tap Apply on any card to apply the preset immediately. If you have unsaved local changes, a dialog asks whether to save first or apply anyway
If a preset contains device settings, Apply is enabled only when that device type is connected; otherwise it is disabled. You can always import the preset to save it locally for later use
Importing a preset

Import saves the preset as a named local entry in your library, so you can switch back to it any time — just like a locally created preset. Requires a Cloud Pro subscription, or the Hemiola Pro in-app purchase.
Tap Import, choose a name (pre-filled with the preset name), and confirm
The imported preset is added to every relevant section (Analog, FX tiers, MIDI Mapper) that it contains
Last Used panel
The last preset you applied or imported is pinned at the bottom of the screen for quick re-apply and voting.
Tap the play button to re-apply the preset without scrolling back to it
Tap the download button to import it (requires subscription)
Use the thumbs-up / thumbs-down buttons on any preset card or in the Last Used panel to vote. Tapping the same vote again removes it. Voting requires a cloud account but not a paid subscription
Creator Tiers
Active Cloud Pro creators earn tier badges based on the total number of likes their published presets receive. Higher tiers give a small score boost in the Trending ranking.
🟢 Sound Designer — 500+ likes
🔥 Master Producer — 2,500+ likes
👑 Hemiola Legend — 10,000+ likes
Tier badges appear next to the author name on preset cards. The badge and its score boost are only active while the creator holds a Cloud Pro subscription.
Bookmarks
Bookmark presets you want to find again quickly. Requires a Cloud Pro subscription. Bookmarks are synced across your devices.
Tap the bookmark icon on any preset card to add or remove it from your bookmarks
Use the Bookmarks filter button at the top of the library to show only your bookmarked presets
Publishing your presets
Share your own sounds with the community. Requires a Cloud Pro subscription.
Open the Preset dialog for any supported section and tap Publish…
Choose which sections to include (Analog, FX tiers, Device), give the preset a name and optional description, and add hashtags to help others find it
Your published presets appear in the Shared Presets tab of the Cloud Account dialog, where you can edit, hide, or delete them
Enable Public Profile (Profile tab in Cloud Account) to make your presets visible to the community. Presets that are marked public but whose author profile is not public show a warning icon.

Push Notifications let Hemiola reach you with announcements, offers, updates, and system messages. Notifications are delivered in real time when the app is open. On reconnection, any missed notifications are fetched automatically from the server.
Bell Icon

The bell icon in the app bar is visible for both signed-in users and guests. A red badge shows the number of unread notifications. Tap the icon to open the notifications panel, which expands directly below the icon.
Unread notifications are shown with a filled circle; read notifications with an empty circle
Tap a notification to expand it and read the full content
Tap the settings icon in the top-right corner of the popup to open Cloud Account and manage notification preferences
Tap the dismiss button (×) on a notification to hide it from the list
Tap Mark All Read to mark all visible notifications as read at once
Tap Show All to open the full notifications page with the complete history
Cross-Device Sync
When signed in, notification read and dismissed state is synchronised across all your devices. In guest mode, notification state is tracked per device only and is not synchronised to other devices.
A notification you have already dismissed will not reappear, even if the same notification is re-sent by the server.
The Preferences dialog is accessible from the Home screen (tap Hemiola logo → Preferences). It contains app-wide settings organized in sections.
General
Language: choose the app language. The system language is used by default.
Keep Screen On: prevents the device screen from dimming while the app is in use (mobile only).
Controls Autopilot: automatically navigate to affected controls when MIDI parameters change via built-in mappings.
Keyboard Autopilot: show and scroll the keyboard when MIDI notes are received.
Cloud
Stay Signed On: when enabled, the app keeps you logged in across sessions.
General MIDI Prefs
SF Channel: MIDI channel for the SoundFont synth (default: OMNI).
Analog Channel: MIDI channel for the Analog synth (default: OMNI).
MIDI Thru: forward incoming MIDI messages to connected output devices.
Pitch Bend Range: sensitivity of incoming pitch bend in semitones.
Factory Reset
Erases all saved preferences, presets, and cloud device registration. The app closes and restarts fresh on the next launch.
Factory Reset cannot be undone. Your cloud account and published presets are not affected — only the local data on this device is erased.
The Privacy tab in Cloud Account controls how Hemiola handles anonymous device usage data. No personal data is ever collected or transmitted.
Anonymous Device Data

When enabled, Hemiola reports anonymous statistics about the MIDI devices you connect — such as device name, firmware version, and connection type. This data is used to prioritise plugin development and improve compatibility.
Data is always hashed and anonymised — it cannot be traced back to you
No preset content, audio, or personal information is ever included
You can opt in or out at any time using the toggle in the Privacy tab
Known Devices
The Known Devices list shows the devices whose anonymous data has been reported to the server. You can review them, and delete all reported data if you wish.
Tap Delete All to permanently remove all device data from the server
After deletion the list will be empty until new data is reported (if sharing is enabled)
You are shown a consent dialog the first time you sign in. Your choice is saved, but you can always change it in the Privacy tab.

The Audio Calibration Wizard measures the round-trip audio latency of your device and evaluates the quality of your setup for live performance. It runs automatically on first launch and can be re-run at any time from Home → Calibrate.
What Is Measured
Latency (ms): the round-trip time between a note trigger and the audible sound. Measured by playing a test tone and detecting its echo via the microphone.
Stability: how consistent the latency is across multiple measurements. A stable setup maintains the same latency without spikes.
Polyphony: how many simultaneous voices your device can sustain without audio glitches (XRuns). Lower-end devices may be limited to 3–5 voices.
Native Sample Rate: the hardware output rate (typically 44.1 kHz or 48 kHz). Hemiola automatically matches this to avoid resampling, which would degrade latency.
Latency Grades
Pro Grade (< 12 ms): transparent, suitable for live keyboard playing.
Stable (12–25 ms): slightly noticeable at high tempos; acceptable for most users.
Risky (> 25 ms): audible delay. Consider using wired headphones, closing background apps, or lowering the audio buffer size in Settings → Audio.
Polyphony Grades
Full (8 voices): no restriction; all voices active.
Reduced (5 voices): older or thermal-throttled devices. The voice cap is applied automatically.
Limited (3 voices): performance-constrained device. Dense polyphony will be capped.
Frame Rate Tier
Fast (P90 < 16.67 ms): the GPU/UI pipeline runs at 60 fps. All visual effects are active.
Marginal (16.67–33 ms): roughly 30 fps. Some visual effects are automatically suppressed to protect audio performance.
Slow (P90 ≥ 33 ms): the device cannot sustain real-time audio and full UI simultaneously. Heavy visual effects are disabled.

Frame Rate Fails: if the frame tier is Marginal or Slow, Hemiola automatically reduces visual complexity to keep audio glitch-free. Re-running calibration after a device restart often improves the result. On old and/or slow devices Hemiola might not perform correctly: try with a more recent and/or powerful device.
Calibration Report Card

After the wizard completes, a Report Card is shown with device model, platform, all grades, and a 'Ready for Stage' or 'Potential Issues' status. You can re-open this card at any time by tapping the latency tachometer on the Home screen.

If Results Are Poor: a link to the online help page is shown when critical issues are detected. Common causes include background apps competing for audio resources, a non-native sample rate, or a device with hardware limitations.
Tips for Better Results
Use wired headphones or a USB audio interface — they bypass the internal DAC and typically halve the latency.
Close all other apps before calibrating, especially streaming services and communication apps.
On Android, grant the RECORD_AUDIO permission. Without it, the latency measurement cannot run.
Re-run calibration after connecting or disconnecting audio hardware — the native sample rate may change.
Live Mode prepares your device for a live performance. It checks all the conditions that could disrupt a performance on stage and lets you activate the audio engine protections in one tap.
Activating Live Mode
Tap the LIVE button on the Home screen to open the Live Mode dialog. The checklist shows the current status of your setup. When all checks pass (or only warnings are present), tap Activate.
On Android, Activate requests exclusive Audio Focus, preventing other apps from playing audio and muting WhatsApp, music players, and similar interruptions.
The synth engine starts automatically if it was not already running.
The LIVE button turns accent-coloured with a border highlight while Live Mode is active.
Checklist Items
Audio Engine: shows the measured latency grade from the last calibration. A Pro Grade result is ideal. If no calibration has been run, this shows '—'.
MIDI Device: shows whether a MIDI controller is connected and its name. Orange means no device — you can still perform using the on-screen keyboard.
Do Not Disturb: orange if DND is off. Enable DND from your device's notification bar before performing.
Battery: orange if below 30%. Charge before a performance to avoid audio drop-outs caused by power management throttling.
Charging: orange if a charger is connected. Chargers can introduce ground hum into the audio path — unplug before performing if you hear noise.
Thermal State: orange (warning) or red (critical) if the device is hot. Thermal throttling degrades audio performance.
Deactivating Live Mode
Tap the ACTIVE button to reopen the dialog, then tap Deactivate. On Android this releases the Audio Focus, allowing other apps to play audio again.
Audio Interruptions

Even with Live Mode active, certain system events can interrupt audio on Android. Hemiola handles these gracefully: all active notes are silenced immediately (MIDI Panic Soft) and a non-blocking banner appears.
Best practice for live performance: activate Airplane Mode or Do Not Disturb before going on stage, unplug the charger, and ensure the device is sufficiently charged (>50%).
The Let's Play! popup appears every time Hemiola launches and lets you jump straight into playing with a single tap.
The Piano — opens the Piano Keyboard tab ready to play.
The Drums — opens the Piano Keyboard tab in drum-pad mode.
A Midi Keyboard — opens the Devices tab and starts a 10-second auto-connect window that automatically connects the first new MIDI device found.
A Wind Controller — same as Midi Keyboard; ideal for breath controllers and wind MIDI devices.
Don't Ask Again
Check the Don't Ask Again box before tapping a choice or Cancel to permanently suppress the popup. You can re-enable it at any time via Preferences → Factory Reset → Reset Quick Start.
